description:
docs/SPEC-lifecycle-sync-participants.md §12 (line ~306: 'the call-site rewire is bl-2bf7') and §14 step 7 ('bl-2bf7 (apply to review/close). Subscribes participants ... failure-policy machinery') describe bl-2bf7 as the ball that wires participant failure-policy into the lifecycle COMMANDS. bl-2bf7 (delivered, main 6b2edfb) actually delivered only the GIT-REMOTE participant slice: state-branch sync + required-rollback on review/close, plus the --sync/--no-sync flags. It did NOT rewire the plugin dispatch call sites — cmd_review/close/update/create still discard plugin::dispatch_push's result on main, and there is no §11 override log or sync_status. So the SPEC over-claims bl-2bf7's scope and leaves the plugin-side command consumption looking done when it is not.

FIX: in §12 and §14, state bl-2bf7's real (git-remote) scope accurately and point the remaining PLUGIN-participant command-level consumption (rollback/non-zero on required plugin failure or Reject; EventCtx task_before+commit threading; §11 plugin override log + sync_status; --skip/--required surface) at bl-fb4d. Do NOT rewrite the rest of the §14 historical migration plan — bl-d31c/eae4/1ea6/b1dd/50c5/4e7d/8b71/a46d all delivered (verified: [bl-xxxx] squash commits on main); their refs are accurate provenance in original planning tense, intentionally left. WHY: a SPEC that says work is tracked/done when it is not is the exact failure mode that nearly closed bl-cc24 with the plugin-side gap invisible.
title: SPEC accuracy: bl-2bf7 references misattribute plugin-side scope; point remainder to bl-fb4d
